title: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. (GS) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: Opus 4.6 independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Goldman Sachs Group Inc. (GS). Economic Prospect Score: 68/100 — Moderate Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. is projected to grow FCF at 7% annually, reflecting its competitive position, sector dynamics, and historical compounding rate.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 10.5% WACC balances Goldman Sachs Group Inc.'s earnings quality and balance sheet strength against sector volatility and macroeconomic risk factors.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.5% terminal rate assumes long-run growth converges toward nominal GDP, appropriate for a mature S&P 500 constituent.

thesisTitle: Goldman Sachs Group Inc.: A Fundamental Assessment
thesisParagraphs: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. (GS) is a large-cap constituent of the S&P 500 with established market position and consistent cash flow generation. The company's competitive advantages and market share provide a degree of pricing power and earnings stability that supports a premium valuation.

My DCF applies a 7% FCF growth rate reflecting the company's historical compounding ability and forward outlook, with a 10.5% discount rate capturing sector-specific risks and capital market conditions. The valuation is sensitive to assumptions about sustainable growth — investors should stress-test these inputs against their own convictions.
verdictFooter: Opus 4.6 independent analysis. Data via public filings (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: What is Goldman Sachs Group Inc.'s intrinsic value according to Opus?
faq.answer: Based on a 10-year DCF with 7% FCF growth and 10.5% WACC, Opus calculates GS's intrinsic value at approximately $643.63 per share, suggesting the stock is fairly valued at $590.00.

faq.question: What assumptions drive GS's valuation?
faq.answer: The key drivers are the FCF growth rate (7%) and discount rate (10.5%). A 1% increase in growth adds roughly 10-15% to the intrinsic value. These assumptions reflect Opus's independent assessment of the company's competitive position and risk profile.

faq.question: Should I buy or sell based on this analysis?
faq.answer: This is not financial advice. DCF models are inherently subjective — small changes in assumptions create large swings in output. Use this as one data point among many in your investment research process.

prospectVerdictDetail: Goldman is the premier investment bank with a refocused strategy after exiting consumer banking (Marcus). Asset management and wealth management provide more stable revenue. Trading and advisory are cyclical but Goldman dominates the league tables.
prospectKeyRisks: Investment banking revenue volatility — M&A and IPO markets can freeze in a recession
Trading revenue normalizing from elevated post-COVID levels
Loss of consumer banking ambitions (Marcus retreat) reducing diversification narrative
prospectKeyCatalysts: M&A and IPO activity recovery from depressed 2023-2024 levels driving investment banking fee rebound
Asset management and alternatives AUM growing as Goldman expands beyond institutional
Platform Solutions (transaction banking) growing as Goldman builds a fintech competitor
prospectMethodology: Score is based on three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30), totaling 0-100. Each pillar is broken into individually scored factors with transparent rationale. Data sources include FY2025 10-K filings, analyst consensus estimates, news sentiment analysis, and competitive landscape assessment. The score is forward-looking and represents economic prospect over a 2-3 year horizon.
